title: Pre-launch restaurant with only demographic and rent data
prompt: I have not opened yet. I know the neighborhood population, monthly rent, and the names of five nearby restaurants. Tell me whether a 40-seat dinner restaurant will be profitable and how many daily orders it will get.

patterns: no operating baseline|pre[- ]launch.*(?:proxy|comparable)|没有.*历史.*基准|未开业.*代理
seat.*turn|table turn|40.*seat|座位.*翻台|座位.*翻桌
permit|licen[cs]e|food safety|牌照|许可证|食品安全
pop[- ]?up|deposit|pre[- ]?sale|paid pilot|快闪|快閃|订金|訂金|预售|預售

critical_failures: Invents daily orders from population alone.
Declares profitability without food, labor, capacity, and price inputs.
human_rubric: Does the response use comparables as proxies rather than facts about the new restaurant?
Does it define a pre-opening experiment with thresholds?
